# ForgeOS Depthscope — Session Handoff

## Project Overview
ForgeOS is a clean-room Rust backtest engine for crypto microstructure edge discovery. The #1 rule is the null-edge gate: coinflip must lose ~fees, or the engine is lying. The current focus is depthscope — a tool that replays L2 orderbook data and computes depth features at macrostructure timescales (15min-4hr holds).

## Team
- **Trader**: 10y discretionary macrostructure trader, NOT a coder. Wants honest verdicts, simple trading terms. Method: chart pattern TRIGGER + orderflow CONFIRM.
- **GLM-5.1 (you)**: Engine architecture, Rust code, honesty gates, null-edge validation. The skeptic.
- **DeepSeek V4 Pro**: Data analysis, feature prototyping, ML/statistical work, Python scripts. The quick thinker.

## WHERE TO FIND EVERYTHING

### GitHub Repo
- Remote: `github.com:addineb/forgeOS.git`
- Branch: `depth-pattern`
- Clone: `git clone git@github.com:addineb/forgeOS.git && cd forgeOS && git checkout depth-pattern`
- Push access: you have it. Commit and push your changes.

### Obsidian Vault (trader's notes, research, decisions)
- Location: `C:\Users\User\Desktop\obsidian\forgeos\`
- This is where the trader captures ideas, research notes, and decisions
- Key files to read for full context:
  - `docs/START-HERE.md` — project entry point
  - `docs/ForgeOS-HOME.md` — project home/overview
  - `docs/PROJECT-CONTEXT.md` — current project context and priorities

  - `docs/research/` — all research docs (lag, OI, VP, sweep, etc.)
  - `docs/engine-design.md` — engine design notes
  - `docs/migration-from-wallbot.md` — why we rebuilt from scratch

### Code Structure (in workspace `C:\Users\User\.kiro\forgeOS\`)
- `crates/depthscope/src/main.rs` — THE MAIN FILE you'll be working on
- `crates/forge-depth/src/features.rs` — DepthFeatures struct, compute(), csv_header(), to_csv_row()
- `crates/forge-depth/src/depth.rs` — DepthSnapshot, ask_vol_at_band(), bid_vol_at_band()
- `crates/forge-depth/src/cvd.rs` — CVD tracker
- `crates/forge-depth/src/volume_profile.rs` — VolumeProfile
- `crates/forge-depth/src/wall_tracker.rs` — WallTracker
- `crates/forge-book/src/book.rs` — OrderBook (the core book engine)
- `crates/forge-core/src/` — Price, Qty, Side, Event, EventKind, UnixNanos
- `crates/forge-data/src/` — data reading/writing
- `crates/forgelag/` — the old lagshot study (shelved)
- `crates/forge-sim/` — simulation engine
- `crates/forge-strategy/` — strategy framework
- `tools/` — Python analysis scripts

### Hetzner Box (where the data and builds live)
- SSH: `root@167.233.57.140`
- Rust: 1.96.0 (run `source ~/.cargo/env` first)
- Data: `/root/chd/data/ticks/BTCUSDT/bookDelta/` and `/root/chd/data/ticks/BTCUSDT/trade/`
- Build: `source ~/.cargo/env && cd /root/forgeOS && cargo build --release -p depthscope`
- Binary: `/root/forgeOS/target/release/depthscope`
- Deploy changed files: `scp <local_file> root@167.233.57.140:/root/forgeOS/<path>` then rebuild

### Memory Files (persistent notes across sessions)
- `/memories/forgeos-notes.md` — project summary, current state, team structure
- `/memories/community-sources.md` — repos to scrap ideas from
- `/memories/repo/depthscope-status.md` — depthscope feature list and status

## Current State (as of 2026-06-16)

### Depthscope — What's Built and Working
Binary tool at `crates/depthscope/src/main.rs`. Library at `crates/forge-depth/src/`.

**57 features per snapshot:**
- Depth shape: full_imbalance, top5_imbalance, weighted_imbalance, spread_bps, bid/ask levels, total vol
- Depth concentration: ask/bid_concentration (top-3/total)
- Inter-level gaps: best_ask/bid_gap_bps, mean_ask/bid_gap_bps
- CVD: delta, ratio, count_imbalance, momentum, acceleration
- Volume profile: poc_price, va_high, va_low, concentration, mid_to_poc_bps
- Wall tracking: active_wall_count, wall_cancel_ratio, avg_wall_lifetime_s, bid/ask_wall_vol
- Depth bands: V1/V3/V5/V10/V20/V50/V100 per side (14 columns)
- Depth band ratios: ask/bid_conc_ratio, ask/bid_depth_skew, cross_ask_ratio, depth_breadth_ask/bid (7 columns)
- Price context: mid_price, best_bid, best_ask, cum_vol
- Forward returns: fwd_ret_15m_bps, fwd_ret_1h_bps, fwd_ret_4h_bps

**Two modes:**
- Time-bar: `--interval-s 60` (snapshot every 60s)
- Volume-bar: `--volume-bar 10` (snapshot every 10 BTC traded)

**CLI flags:**
- `--data-root`, `--symbol`, `--date`, `--hours`, `--interval-s`, `--volume-bar`, `--warmup-s`, `--wall-threshold`, `--vp-bin-width`, `--vp-window-s` (default 300), `--top-n`, `--output` (supports `{symbol}`, `{date}`, `{mode}` placeholders), `--feed-latency-ns`

**Data quality checks (just added, NOT yet deployed to Hetzner):**
- Crossed book warning at each snapshot
- Low level count warning (<50 levels per side after warmup)
- Data quality summary at end: crossed_trims, evicted, final levels

**Known issues:**
- Forward returns are per-date only (4hr return at 20:00 UTC = 0 if no next-day data). Solution: Python post-processing to stitch dates.
- CHD data has no initial snapshots, only deltas. 5-min warmup workaround (book converges to 1100+ levels per side).

### What's on Hetzner (NOT yet deployed with latest integrity checks)
- Last deployed: volume bar dedup fix + VP window fix + output placeholders + cum_vol + depth band ratios
- Need to deploy: book integrity checks (crossed book, low level warnings)
- Command: `scp C:\Users\User\.kiro\forgeOS\crates\depthscope\src\main.rs root@167.233.57.140:/root/forgeOS/crates/depthscope/src/main.rs` then rebuild

### Available Data
- 17 BTC dates on Hetzner at `/root/chd/data/ticks/BTCUSDT/bookDelta/` and `/root/chd/data/ticks/BTCUSDT/trade/`
- Dates: 2025-12-01, 2025-12-15, 2026-01-15, 2026-02-01, 2026-03-01, 2026-04-01/02/03, 2026-05-01, 2026-06-02 through 06-10
- NO ETH data yet (DeepSeek's job to pull)

## What Needs To Be Done Next (Priority Order)

### 1. Deploy integrity checks and run full dataset (IMMEDIATE)
- Deploy latest main.rs to Hetzner (has book integrity checks)
- Build: `ssh root@167.233.57.140 "source ~/.cargo/env && cd /root/forgeOS && cargo build --release -p depthscope"`
- Run all 17 BTC dates with volume-bar mode (10 BTC bars): create a shell script on Hetzner that loops over all dates
- Example command per date: `./target/release/depthscope --data-root /root/chd/data/ticks --symbol BTCUSDT --date 2026-06-09 --volume-bar 10 --warmup-s 300 --output /root/depthscope_out/BTCUSDT_2026-06-09_vb10.csv`
- Also run time-bar mode (60s) for comparison: `--interval-s 60 --output /root/depthscope_out/BTCUSDT_2026-06-09_tb60s.csv`

### 2. Cross-date forward returns (Python post-processing)
- The 4hr forward return at 20:00 UTC is 0 because there's no next-day data loaded
- Write a Python script that:
  1. Reads all per-date CSVs in order
  2. For each snapshot, finds the closest future snapshot at 15min/1hr/4hr ahead
  3. Recomputes forward returns using the next date's data
- This is straightforward — just merge the CSVs by timestamp and look up mid_price

### 3. Pull ETHUSDT data (DeepSeek's job)
- Need ETH data for cross-venue comparison
- CHD has ETHUSDT data available

### 4. Start depth-pattern analysis
- With 17 days × ~2000 volume bars = ~34,000 data points
- Key questions:
  - Do depth band ratios (conc_ratio, depth_skew) predict forward returns?
  - Does CVD momentum predict direction at 15min-4hr?
  - Are there regime shifts in depth structure that precede big moves?
  - Can we find a depth pattern that clears the ~9bps fee bar?

### 5. Engine audit (trader reminder)
- After depthscope work, trader wants a full review of the Opus-built engine crates
- Check forge-core, forge-data, forge-book for correctness

## Key Lessons from Prior Work
- Binding constraint: ~9bps taker fee vs ~5bps microstructure edge (signals real but smaller than toll)
- LAGSHOT: real edge but NOT capturable at retail latency (~1-2.4s). Shelved.
- OI-CASCADE: real cascades but naive fade sub-fee. Exhausted.
- SWEEP/STOP-RUN: real setup, big moves (45-126bps), BUT orderflow confirm doesn't separate reversal from continuation. No trade clears net-positive at trustable n.
- VP LVN: coin flip alone; orderflow confirm (absorption) failed to separate.
- LADDER: per-trade positive but per-euro NEGATIVE (size illusion). CLOSED.
- Durable wins: exhaustion/flow-deceleration (directional read), structural wick-stop, reclaim entry, OKX reference venue
- NEXT lead: must be >>9bps per trade, latency-robust, NOT symmetric mean-reversion

## Sweep Study Context (from docs/research/sweep-study.md)
- Sweeps are REAL, moves are BIG (45-126bps mean), BUT orderflow confirm (top-5 depth imbalance absorption) does NOT separate reversal from continuation
- This is WHY we're doing depth-pattern study — need deeper features than top-5 imbalance
- The community info dump about desync risk, sequence numbers, checksums, and snapshot+delta architecture has been reviewed. Key takeaways for our backtesting case:
  - We don't need live reconnection (we replay from parquet)
  - We DO need book integrity checks (crossed book, low levels) — just added
  - We should consider adding sequence number gap detection if CHD provides sequence IDs
  - Checksum validation would be ideal but CHD doesn't provide exchange checksums

## Build/Test Commands
- Local check: `cargo check -p depthscope`
- Local test: `cargo test --release`
- Hetzner build: `ssh root@167.233.57.140 "source ~/.cargo/env && cd /root/forgeOS && cargo build --release -p depthscope"`
- Hetzner test: `ssh root@167.233.57.140 "source ~/.cargo/env && cd /root/forgeOS && cargo test --release"`
- Cross-compile from Windows: DOES NOT WORK (zstd-sys needs Linux gcc). Build on Hetzner instead.
- Git push: `git push origin depth-pattern`
